question_en: In what year did World War I end?
context_en: World War I ended on 11 November 1918 with the signing of the Armistice of Compiègne. The conflict caused more than 20 million deaths.

question_en: Who was the first emperor of Rome?
context_en: Augustus (Octavian) was the first Roman emperor, ruling from 27 BC to AD 14. Julius Caesar was never officially 'emperor'.

question_en: In what year did the Berlin Wall fall?
context_en: The Berlin Wall fell on 9 November 1989, a symbol of the end of the Cold War and the start of German reunification.
Machu Picchu was built by the Inca Empire in the 15th century as a residence for emperor Pachacútec, at 2,430 m of altitude.

question_en: In what year did Christopher Columbus reach America?
context_en: On 12 October 1492, Columbus reached Guanahaní (Bahamas) believing he had reached Asia. It marked the start of the colonial era.

question_en: In what year did World War II end?
context_en: World War II ended in 1945: in Europe on 8 May (V-E Day) and in the Pacific on 2 September, after Japan's surrender.

question_en: Who was the first president of the United States?
context_en: George Washington was the first U.S. president (1789-1797). He was the military leader during the American Revolution.

question_en: In what year did the French Revolution begin?
context_en: The French Revolution began in 1789 with the storming of the Bastille on 14 July. It proclaimed the ideals of liberty, equality and fraternity.

options_en: A general with no political office
context_en: Napoleon was Emperor of the French (1804-1815). He conquered much of Europe before being defeated at Waterloo.

question_en: In which battle was Napoleon definitively defeated?
context_en: At Waterloo (1815), the allied forces of Wellington and Blücher defeated Napoleon, who was exiled to Saint Helena where he died in 1821.

question_en: Which empire was known as 'the empire on which the sun never sets'?
context_en: The British Empire at its peak (19th century) covered 24% of the Earth's land. The phrase was earlier attributed to the Spanish Empire.
